title: Restore the analyst run-summary sentinel: fix the dispatcher-wide summary-write gap + the monitor/analysis vs monitor/analyst path typo + close tinker's pipeline-health blind spot.
depends_on: PROP-022 Phase 1 (FUSE-canonical state files — established the pattern of which path is authoritative)
PROP-030 (queue-history backlog-trend; tinker's pipeline-health audit gets a parallel cross-check added here)
PROP-034 Phase 1 (baby-analyst summary-write pattern — the template I am recommending analyst adopt)
headline_recommendation: Adopt Option D (hybrid): (1) add an unconditional 'Write run summary' postlude as the final numbered step in analyst.md so every mode writes the same sentinel; (2) fix the monitor/analysis/ → monitor/analyst/ path typo in three files; (3) add a substantive-output cross-check to tinker's pipeline-health audit so a broken sentinel can never silently hide a healthy analyst again. Self-apply NONE of these — operator reviews and approves first; then tinker (or operator) applies the prompt edits in a single commit. Verifiable post-fix by reading monitor/analyst/latest-analysis-summary.txt mtime on the next analyst run.

executive_summary.problem_one_line: monitor/analyst/latest-analysis-summary.txt has been frozen at 2026-04-25T07:04Z for 19 days despite the analyst running healthy work the entire time (≥20 tracker entries flipped to status=complete since 2026-05-13T12:00Z alone, plus 14 fresh issue-proposals); the operator's primary 'is the analyst alive?' signal has been silently broken and tinker's pipeline-health audit didn't catch it.
executive_summary.root_cause_one_line: Two pre-existing bugs that collided when dome went quiet: (1) the V6 dispatcher refactor (commit 713f800, 2026-04-08, '437-line monolith → 110-line dispatcher + 5 worker modules') left the explicit summary-write step ONLY inside analyst-normal-analysis.md — Modes 0/1/1b/2/2b/3/4/5 have no summary-write step; the analyst.md:284 line 'All other modes: write summary and stop' is too vague (no path, no schema) to function as an enforced contract; (2) the surviving summary-write step in analyst-normal-analysis.md writes to the typo path monitor/analysis/ (with -is) instead of the canonical monitor/analyst/ (with -t), and monitor/analysis/ is not in git, not in workspace-sync's manifest, so all Normal-mode summary writes since 2026-04-08 have orphaned in FUSE. The 19-day staleness started when dome's cadence-rollup HOLD pattern stopped triggering Normal mode (the only mode that wrote anything, even to the wrong path) — leaving the only mode that ever wrote at all idle, while the other 8 modes continued doing real work invisibly.
executive_summary.fix_one_line: Add an unconditional 'Write run summary' postlude (numbered step) to analyst.md that mirrors curmudgeon.md Step 9 and analyst-baby.md Step 10; fix the path typo in all three affected reference files; add a substantive-output mtime cross-check to tinker's pipeline-health staleness probe so the next similar bug surfaces immediately.
executive_summary.scope_clarification: Design only. Operator reviews PROP, then either tinker self-applies the prompt edits (under PROP-014-amendment-001 Q6 lifecycle) or operator routes via a separate directive. NO scheduled-task change. NO scheduling-cadence change (PROP-034 Phase 1 schedules unchanged). The three prompt edits (analyst.md postlude, analyst-normal-analysis.md path fix, decider-intake.md path fix, tinker-infrastructure.md path fix, tinker-pipeline-health.md cross-check) are all in git-owned files that fall under the prompt-edits-via-PROP path, not NEVER_PUSH.

context_verification.git_canonical_summary_last_workspace_syn: 2026-05-05T19:05:58Z (commit e0c87c7 — workspace-sync push of the already-stale Apr-25 content; smart_copy hashed-equal short-circuit didn't fire because monitor/analyst/latest-analysis-summary.txt is in workspace-sync's explicit smart_copy list and the hash matched, so it was re-touched but content didn't change)
context_verification.git_typo_dir_evidence: GET /repos/funwithscience-org/dome-model-review/contents/monitor/analysis → 404 Not Found. monitor/analysis/ has never been pushed to git. All Normal-mode writes since 2026-04-08 have orphaned in FUSE.
context_verification.operator_smoking_gun_1_confirmed: Yes. grep -rn 'monitor/analysis/' monitor/prompts/ → 6 occurrences in 3 files:

monitor/prompts/reference/analyst-normal.context: Step 7 unconditional end-of-run summary write target

monitor/prompts/reference/decider-intake.context: Decider reads the analyst's latest-analysis-summary as part of its intake — also using the typo path

monitor/prompts/reference/tinker-infrast.context: Mode 2 error-pattern grep over monitor/analysis/*.json (would scan zero files since dir is FUSE-orphan; not catastrophic, just dead)
Mode 2 fallback grep over monitor/analysis/*.json — same dead-scan

context_verification.operator_smoking_gun_2_confirmed: Yes. Per-mode audit table below — Normal mode is the ONLY mode that prescribes a summary write. The analyst.md:284 line ('All other modes: write summary and stop') is informational, not procedural: no path, no schema, no enforcement, and the per-mode reference files don't echo it.

per_mode_summary_write_audit.conditional_or_unconditional: Step 1 no-op short-circuit writes 'No pending changes' and stops; Step 7 writes unconditionally at end of Normal flow
informational sentence 'All other modes: write summary and stop' — no path, no schema, not enforced

operator_smoking_gun_3_root_cause_commit.commit_title: Analyst dispatcher architecture: 437 lines → 110-line dispatcher + 5 worker modules
operator_smoking_gun_3_root_cause_commit.interpretation: The pre-V6 monolithic analyst.md (437 lines) presumably had a summary-write that applied to every mode. The V6 refactor sliced the monolith into a 110-line dispatcher + 5 per-mode worker modules — and only one worker (Normal mode) inherited an explicit summary-write step. The other 4 worker modules + the dispatcher itself only retained a vague 'write summary and stop' phrase with no path or enforcement. So the gap predates 2026-04-25 by 17 days; the visibility-trigger was dome going quiet around 2026-04-25, not a code change at that date. Hypothesis from the directive ('PROP-022 Phase 1 refactor on 2026-04-25 dropped the write') is REFUTED: PROP-022 Phase 1 (FUSE-canonical attention-inbox) is a Mode 2b change and didn't touch the per-mode summary-write contract — which had already been absent for 17 days.
supporting_evidence: GitHub commits API: monitor/prompts/reference/analyst-normal-analysis.md → 3 commits, oldest 713f800 (2026-04-08), newest d923315 (2026-04-10)
GitHub commits API: monitor/prompts/reference/analyst-mode1-expansions.md → 9 commits, oldest 713f800 (2026-04-08), newest 2595303 (2026-05-13 PROP-034)
GitHub commits API: monitor/prompts/reference/analyst-mode34-procedures.md → 1 commit, 94cea58 (2026-04-08, PROP-001/002 prompt diet)
Aggregate: every analyst reference file was authored 2026-04-08 ±2 days; none were touched in 2026-04-15→2026-05-02 (the directive's suspect window)

operator_smoking_gun_4_visibility_trigge.interpretation: Before Apr 25, Normal mode fired on most analyst runs (steady stream of poll-time dome changes from dome's normal commit cadence). The Normal-mode summary-write to monitor/analysis/ kept happening even though the path was wrong — at least the FUSE-orphan file kept getting a fresh mtime, which inspection from inside the workspace could see. Around Apr 25, dome shifted into the cadence-rollup HOLD pattern (no substantive predictions changes). Normal mode stopped firing as the dominant mode. Other modes (Mode 1 expansion drain, Mode 2b inbox processing) picked up the slack — but those modes don't write the summary. The CANONICAL-path file froze at Apr 25 (last Normal-mode-or-equivalent run that touched it); the TYPO-path file kept advancing until 2026-05-04 (last Normal-mode poll-time write). After May 4, NEITHER path was advancing — both sentinels were silent while analyst kept running substantive work via Modes 1 / 2b.
operator_smoking_gun_4_visibility_trigge.asymmetry_between_canonical_apr25_and_ty: The asymmetry (canonical-path stuck at Apr-25, typo-path advancing through May-4) suggests there's a SECOND, undocumented write path that briefly wrote to canonical-path around Apr-25 — possibly the analyst.md:284 'All other modes: write summary and stop' line being interpreted by the analyst LLM as a request to write to a canonical path, or a now-removed Mode-2b step. Without more git history, the exact mechanism can't be pinned. Either way, the dispatcher gap + path typo are the dominant bugs; this asymmetry is a curiosity, not a third bug.

tinker_pipeline_health_blind_spot.consequence: Even though the broken sentinel was 19 days stale, tinker's pipeline-health audit never flagged analyst as stalled — because tinker doesn't probe this file. The operator caught it manually (DIRECTIVE-20260514-001) after the Phase 1 baby launch where a broken sentinel could plausibly have hidden a coordination bug for hours.
tinker_pipeline_health_blind_spot.fix_dimensions: Two-pronged: (1) ADD monitor/analyst/latest-analysis-summary.txt to tinker.md line 121 probe list; (2) ADD a substantive-output cross-check (probe EXP mtimes + issue-proposal mtimes + tracker write mtimes) so a future broken sentinel can't fool tinker even if the file is in the probe list.

option_A_dispatcher_postlude.summary: Add a 'Write run summary' postlude block to analyst.md that runs unconditionally at end of every mode. Path: canonical monitor/analyst/latest-analysis-summary.txt. Mirrors curmudgeon.md Step 9 and analyst-baby.md Step 10.
pros: Single edit point in analyst.md (low surface area)
Mirrors curmudgeon + baby's working patterns — proven shape
Cannot be forgotten when adding a future Mode N (mode dispatcher always falls through to postlude)
Mode-agnostic content schema reduces per-mode prose drift
cons: Slightly less mode-specific verbosity than option B (mitigation: postlude can include conditional 'Mode 1 detail / Mode 1b detail' blocks based on which mode fired)
Requires analyst's dispatcher to RECORD which mode ran (a small bookkeeping addition: set a `mode_fired` variable at dispatch time, reference it in postlude)

option_B_per_mode_step.summary: Add an explicit 'Step N: Write summary' to each of the 7 mode reference files (analyst-mode0/1/1b/2/2b/3/4/5/normal). Allows mode-specific summary content.
pros: Maximum mode-specific verbosity (each mode writes its own most-relevant fields)
cons: 7 files to edit, 7 paths to keep consistent, 7 places to forget — high surface area
Adding a new Mode N in the future means remembering to add a summary-write step, which is exactly the kind of forget-able discipline the V6 refactor already proved we are bad at
Drift risk: per-mode prose will gradually diverge in format, breaking any tool that parses the summary

option_C_drop_sentinel_entirely.summary: Remove monitor/analyst/latest-analysis-summary.txt entirely. Rely on substantive-output mtimes (EXPs, issue-proposals, tracker) as the dashboard signal.
pros: Zero new failure modes (nothing to break)
Substantive-output mtimes are the truer health signal anyway (sentinel can lie; mtimes on EXP files can't)
cons: Loses the human-readable 'what just happened' surface — operator (and tinker reports) consume the sentinel for at-a-glance reading; a `ls -la monitor/analyst/expansions/ | head -10` is a poorer UX
Inconsistent with curmudgeon, decider, baby, integrity, social, tinker — all of whom HAVE a latest-summary file. The pattern exists because it's the right pattern; analyst is the outlier
Tinker's existing pipeline-health docs (CLAUDE.md, reference/tinker-pipeline-health.md) describe sentinel-based audit; dropping analyst's sentinel forces a rewrite of those docs as well

option_D_hybrid.summary: Option A (dispatcher postlude) PLUS extend tinker's pipeline-health audit to (a) probe monitor/analyst/latest-analysis-summary.txt mtime AND (b) cross-check substantive-output mtimes regardless of sentinel state. Defense in depth.
pros: Closes tinker's blind spot — the next similar bug surfaces within one tinker cycle instead of 19 days
Mtimes-as-secondary-signal is cheap (a few stat() calls) and matches the philosophy curmudgeon already uses (review-mtime + tracker-mtime cross-check)
Generalizes: the substantive-output mtime cross-check can be applied to any agent whose sentinel-write contract is fragile, not just analyst
cons: Slightly more tinker prompt surface than pure Option A (mitigation: the cross-check is ~10 lines in tinker-pipeline-health.md)
Two coupled edits per operator-review cycle (analyst.md AND tinker-pipeline-health.md) — minor

Q1_recommended_option.rationale: Option A by itself fixes the immediate symptom but doesn't fix the underlying class of bug (sentinel-write contracts that fail silently). The 19-day visibility gap happened BECAUSE tinker's pipeline-health audit trusts the sentinel mtime as authoritative. Adding the substantive-output cross-check is cheap (~10 lines, a few stat calls per tinker run) and prevents the next 19-day blind spot. CLAUDE.md's tinker section explicitly says 'a broken sentinel write can't silently hide a healthy agent' as the desired property — Option A alone doesn't deliver that; Option D does. The marginal cost over Option A is one paragraph in tinker-pipeline-health.md. Recommend D.

required_fields: run timestamp (ISO-8601 UTC at end-of-run, not at start)
mode(s) fired this run (1-2 entries; Mode 0 can chain to other modes per analyst.md:284)
items processed this run (count + IDs of EXPs authored, issue-proposals written, tracker entries flipped to status=complete, attention-inbox items resolved)
no-op label if no work fired (analogous to the 2026-04-25T07:04Z content already in the canonical file)
next-run carry-over (mode-specific items deferred due to budget or blocked_on dependencies — list IDs)

tracker/inbox state at end-of-run (pending tracker count, pending attention-inbox count, assigned-analyst open-issues count of major/critical/null severity — the same counts tinker probes in PROP-030 backlog-trend)
Q2_minimum_summary_content_for_dashboard.schema_template_minimal:
=== Analyst run <ISO-TS> ===
Mode(s) fired: <Mode list or 'no-op'>
Items processed:
  EXPs authored: <count> (<comma-separated IDs>)
  Issue-proposals: <count> (<IDs>)
  Tracker→complete: <count> (<EXP-IDs>)
  Attention-inbox→resolved: <count> (<inbox-IDs>)
Carry-over: <list-or-'none'>
Queue state: pending-tracker=<N> pending-inbox=<N> assigned-analyst-major+=<N>
Q2_minimum_summary_content_for_dashboard.rationale: These six fields are all already computed in some form during the analyst's work (the EXP files contain authored_at, the tracker contains status updates, the inbox contains resolved_by). Collecting them at end-of-run is a few node-script lines (modeled on analyst-baby.md Step 10). Anything beyond these six fields is per-mode verbosity — let the EXP files themselves carry that.

Q3_tinker_cross_check_when_sentinel_is_f.recommendation: Yes — always cross-check, regardless of sentinel state.
Q3_tinker_cross_check_when_sentinel_is_f.rationale: The 19-day blind spot is the proof. The cost is a few stat() calls per tinker run; the benefit is preventing the next failure mode where the sentinel writes fine but the agent's substantive output has stopped (e.g., analyst's EXP authoring breaks but the postlude still writes 'Mode 1 fired, 0 EXPs authored'). Substantive-output mtimes are the truer health signal; sentinels are the operator-friendly veneer. Both have value; defense in depth uses both.
Q3_tinker_cross_check_when_sentinel_is_f.implementation_sketch: In tinker-pipeline-health.md, after the existing sentinel-mtime probes, add a substantive-output-mtime probe block: for each agent, list 2-3 substantive output paths (e.g., analyst: monitor/analyst/expansions/ newest mtime, monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/ newest mtime; baby: same; decider: monitor/decisions/daily-report-*.json newest mtime, monitor/decisions/applied-patches/ newest mtime). If sentinel is fresh AND substantive output is older than 2× cadence, flag as 'sentinel-only-fresh' — likely a healthy-looking summary masking a broken work pipeline. If sentinel is stale AND substantive output is fresh, flag as 'sentinel-broken-agent-healthy' — the case we just hit.

Q4_path_typo_in_this_prop_or_separate.rationale: The canonical fix (Option D's Option-A component) writes to monitor/analyst/latest-analysis-summary.txt. If the path typo in analyst-normal-analysis.md, decider-intake.md, and tinker-infrastructure.md is not fixed simultaneously, then: (a) Normal mode's existing summary write continues to land at the typo path (FUSE-orphan), creating a TWO-sentinel situation where the new postlude writes to canonical but Normal also writes to typo; (b) decider's intake (reading the typo path) finds stale data permanently; (c) tinker's infrastructure error-grep continues to scan a dead directory. All three fixes are mechanical sed-style replacements (s/monitor\/analysis\//monitor\/analyst\//g in three files); doing them together is one operator-review and one commit.

Q5_baby_summary_coverage.answer: Baby's latest-baby-summary.txt is already covered. Verified.
evidence: analyst-baby.md Step 10 prescribes 'Write run summary to monitor/analyst-baby/latest-baby-summary.txt (workspace-owned)' with explicit content schema (lines 116-122).

tinker-pipeline-health.md line 12 contains PROP-034 Phase 1 baby pipeline health check: 'check monitor/analyst-baby/latest-baby-summary.txt mtime against the 2h cadence. If mtime is older than 4h (2× interval) AND baby's expected work is non-empty (run the babyOwns filter from monitor/prompts/analyst-baby.md dispatcher to confirm there were items to drain), flag as stalled.'
Q5_baby_summary_coverage.additional_protection_from_this_prop: The substantive-output cross-check (Q3) should ALSO apply to baby. Substantive outputs for baby: monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-*-baby-batch-*.json (filename pattern), monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/proposal-*.json filtered by authored_by='analyst-baby'. Add these to the cross-check list.

counter_arguments.operator_directive_signals_evenhanded_ev: DIRECTIVE-20260514-001 task_5 invokes operator preference 'Think about the other side of the argument too' and requires a counter-argument section. Three were enumerated; each handled below.

ca1_broken_sentinel_isnt_hurting_anythin.operator_phrasing: 'Analyst is producing real work — the broken sentinel isn't hurting anything. Fix it only when something downstream actually breaks.'
ca1_broken_sentinel_isnt_hurting_anythin.steelman: Analyst's substantive outputs (EXPs, issue-proposals, tracker writes) are intact. Decider and curmudgeon read EXP files and tracker entries — they don't read latest-analysis-summary.txt for routing decisions (the intake reads it for human-readable context only, per decider-intake.md). The pipeline keeps producing correct outputs. No verdict on the dome model changed. The harm is bounded to operator situational-awareness.
rebuttal: Operator situational awareness is itself a real cost: the directive was filed BECAUSE the operator misread the broken sentinel as 'analyst paused' and spent investigation effort confirming analyst was alive. That's an operator-hour cost, recurring every time the operator scans pipeline health.

The bug masked the PROP-034 Phase 1 baby launch for ~18 hours. If baby had failed to coordinate with analyst (e.g., double-claiming tracker entries), the broken sentinel would have hidden it. The hypothetical didn't materialize this time — but pipeline-health audit fidelity matters most precisely when something is going wrong.

Decider-intake.md line 8 DOES read monitor/analysis/latest-analysis-summary.txt (the typo path). Decider has been reading a 19-day-stale file for human-readable context this whole time. The directive caught the sentinel breakage; this PROP's investigation also caught the decider read-side. That's a second downstream consumer that WAS being hurt.

'Fix it when something breaks' is a triage policy that's fine for high-cost fixes; it's the wrong policy for a 5-line postlude + 6 typo characters. The cost-of-fix is dwarfed by the cost-of-debugging-next-time.

ca2_migrate_normal_mode_pattern_to_each_.operator_phrasing: 'The Normal-mode summary IS the correct pattern. The fix is to migrate Normal-mode style summaries to each mode, not add a postlude.' (i.e. choose Option B, not Option A.)
ca2_migrate_normal_mode_pattern_to_each_.steelman: Per-mode summaries give richer information — Mode 1 can list the EXP IDs and issue resolutions; Mode 1b can list predictions assessed and their verdicts; Mode 2b can list inbox items resolved per phase (OBE/batch/singleton). A postlude collapses all of these into a single uniform template, losing some specificity. Each mode is also self-contained — a new agent or future-tinker reading just analyst-mode1-expansions.md gets the full picture without having to also check analyst.md's postlude.
rebuttal: Surface area: 7 reference files vs 1 dispatcher edit. The V6 refactor exists exactly because high-surface-area prompts are forgotten — we've already proven we can forget a summary-write step under 7-file pressure (we did, on 6 of the 7).

Drift: 7 independently-edited summary templates will diverge over time. The decider-intake or tinker-pipeline-health audit cannot rely on a uniform schema, so any 'parse the summary to extract X' tool has 7 schemas to handle.

Composability: Mode 0 can chain to other modes per analyst.md:284 ('After mode work completes: If Mode 0 completed, also check for Modes 1-4 and normal analysis'). In a chained run, per-mode summaries would either over-write each other or stack non-uniformly. A postlude that gathers all mode-fired flags is the cleaner composition.

Information loss is recoverable: the postlude can include conditional mode-specific blocks ('if Mode 1 fired: list EXP IDs; if Mode 1b fired: list prediction IDs; ...'). Each block is 2-3 lines of node script. This delivers the verbosity of Option B without the surface area.
Curmudgeon (1 summary in main prompt) and baby (1 summary in main prompt) and decider (1 summary in reference file) all use postlude-style; none has felt constrained by it.
ca2_migrate_normal_mode_pattern_to_each_.verdict: Steelman acknowledged. The information-loss concern is real but mitigated by conditional mode-specific blocks in the postlude. Rebuttal holds: fix via Option A's postlude with conditional blocks, NOT 7 per-mode edits.

ca3_per_mode_verbosity_lost_in_postlude.operator_phrasing: 'Per-mode summaries lose information (mode-specific verbosity) when consolidated into a postlude.' (Operator may need to decide on verbosity trade-off.)
ca3_per_mode_verbosity_lost_in_postlude.steelman: An EXP-authoring Mode 1 run has different operationally-useful content from a Mode 2b OBE-triage run. Mode 1 wants 'EXP-NNN authored covering ISS-NNN-OOO with payload size X'; Mode 2b wants 'OBE'd 7 items by ISS-closure / batch-verified 3 / singleton-substantive 1'. Forcing both into a single template loses signal.
rebuttal: Conditional mode-specific blocks in the postlude (see Q2 schema_template_minimal) preserve mode-specific verbosity without per-mode files. Example: if Mode 2b fired, postlude includes 'Mode 2b phases: OBE=N batch=N singleton=N'; if Mode 1 fired, postlude includes 'EXPs authored: list-of-IDs'.

Operationally-useful content for any mode is already in the substantive output files (EXP-NNN.json, proposal-NNN.json, attention-inbox-archive.jsonl). The summary is the at-a-glance 'did the agent run, did it produce work' signal — not the audit trail. Audit trail belongs in the substantive outputs.
Verbosity trade-off is real but smaller than the surface-area trade-off. Operator-readability is best served by a uniform format that conditionally expands per mode.
ca3_per_mode_verbosity_lost_in_postlude.verdict: Steelman acknowledged. Verbosity trade-off accepted; mitigated by conditional blocks. Rebuttal holds: postlude with conditional mode-specific blocks.

verbatim_prompt_edits.note: All edits below are operator-applied or tinker-self-applied under PROP-014-amendment-001 Q6 lifecycle after operator approval. NONE are NEVER_PUSH.

edit_1_analyst_md_postlude.old_block_verbatim:
### After mode work completes:
If Mode 0 completed, also check for Modes 1-4 and normal analysis — Mode 0 doesn't consume the entire run. All other modes: write summary and stop.
edit_1_analyst_md_postlude.new_block_verbatim:
### After mode work completes:
If Mode 0 completed, also check for Modes 1-4 and normal analysis — Mode 0 doesn't consume the entire run. All other modes: fall through to the unconditional postlude below.

### Postlude (unconditional, every run, every mode — added per PROP-036)

This runs at the END of every analyst run regardless of which mode(s) fired, including no-op runs where no mode triggered. Mirrors curmudgeon.md Step 9 and analyst-baby.md Step 10 patterns. The summary is the operator's primary 'is the analyst alive?' signal and tinker's pipeline-health staleness probe.

**Step P1 — Write run summary to `monitor/analyst/latest-analysis-summary.txt`** (overwrite, not append). Required fields:
- Run timestamp (ISO-8601 UTC at end-of-run; use `new Date().toISOString()` at this step, NOT a value cached from dispatch time)
- Mode(s) fired this run (e.g., `Mode 0 → Mode 1`, or `Mode 2b`, or `no-op`)
- Items processed: EXPs authored (count + IDs), issue-proposals written (count + IDs), tracker entries flipped to status=complete this run (count + IDs), attention-inbox items resolved (count + IDs)
- No-op marker if no work fired this run
- Carry-over for next run: items deferred due to budget or blocked_on dependencies
- End-of-run queue state: pending-tracker, pending-inbox, assigned-analyst open-issues count of severity major/critical/null

Minimal schema template (extend per-mode as needed):
```
=== Analyst run <ISO-TS> ===
Mode(s) fired: <Mode-list-or-'no-op'>

Items processed:
  EXPs authored: <count> (<comma-separated IDs>)
  Issue-proposals: <count> (<IDs>)
  Tracker→complete: <count> (<EXP-IDs>)
  Attention-inbox→resolved: <count> (<inbox-IDs>)

Carry-over: <list-or-'none'>

Queue state: pending-tracker=<N> pending-inbox=<N> assigned-analyst-major+=<N>
```

Write via node so you can compute the counts in one place. Example:
```bash
node -e "
const fs=require('fs');
const now=new Date().toISOString();
// ... compute counts from this run's tracker/inbox/expansion deltas ...
const summary = `=== Analyst run ${now} ===\n` +
  `Mode(s) fired: ${MODES_FIRED}\n\n` +
  `Items processed:\n` +
  `  EXPs authored: ${exps.length} (${exps.join(', ')})\n` +
  `  Issue-proposals: ${proposals.length} (${proposals.join(', ')})\n` +
  `  Tracker→complete: ${trackerComplete.length} (${trackerComplete.join(', ')})\n` +
  `  Attention-inbox→resolved: ${inboxResolved.length} (${inboxResolved.join(', ')})\n\n` +
  `Carry-over: ${carryOver.length ? carryOver.join(', ') : 'none'}\n\n` +
  `Queue state: pending-tracker=${pT} pending-inbox=${pI} assigned-analyst-major+=${aA}\n`;
fs.writeFileSync('monitor/analyst/latest-analysis-summary.txt', summary);
console.log('postlude: wrote latest-analysis-summary.txt');
"
```

**This postlude SUPERSEDES the Step 1 short-circuit write and Step 7 unconditional write in analyst-normal-analysis.md** — those two writes are now redundant with the postlude and should be removed (see PROP-036 edit_2).

**Step P2 — Update `monitor/status.json`** with `last_analyst_run: <ISO-TS>` and `last_analyst_mode: <mode-list>`. Use `${WORKSPACE}/monitor/status.json` (FUSE-canonical) to match the rest of the pipeline.
edit_1_analyst_md_postlude.rationale: Establishes the postlude as the single canonical summary-write site. Adds explicit minimum-content schema. Pins the path to canonical monitor/analyst/. Adds Step P2 status.json update so other agents can also detect analyst freshness via status.json (a second-channel signal).

edit_2_analyst_normal_analysis_md_path_f.operation: REPLACE lines 7, 71, 153 (three occurrences of `monitor/analysis/` → `monitor/analyst/`) AND remove the now-redundant Step 1 short-circuit write + Step 7 unconditional write (now superseded by the analyst.md postlude)

edit_2a_line_7_normal_step_1.old_verbatim: Read `monitor/status.json`. If `changes_pending_analysis` is 0 AND no new external reports exist (step 1b), write "No pending changes" to `monitor/analysis/latest-analysis-summary.txt` and stop.
edit_2a_line_7_normal_step_1.new_verbatim: Read `monitor/status.json`. If `changes_pending_analysis` is 0 AND no new external reports exist (step 1b), the dispatcher falls through to the analyst.md postlude which writes the run summary with a no-op marker. Stop here for this mode.
edit_2a_line_7_normal_step_1.rationale: The Step 1 short-circuit no longer needs to write its own summary — the postlude handles no-op summary writing uniformly across all modes.

edit_2b_line_71_normal_step_6.old_verbatim: Create JSON in `monitor/analysis/` with ISO timestamp. Include: kernel_of_truth, forensic_timeline, review_state_crossref, current_review_text_affected, recommended_actions, overall_threat_level, gotchas.
edit_2b_line_71_normal_step_6.new_verbatim: Create JSON in `monitor/analyst/analysis-records/` with ISO timestamp. Include: kernel_of_truth, forensic_timeline, review_state_crossref, current_review_text_affected, recommended_actions, overall_threat_level, gotchas. (The analysis-records subdirectory keeps the per-change records out of the top-level monitor/analyst/ namespace where the operator's eye-scan happens; substantively the same as the prior monitor/analysis/ path but under the canonical analyst dir.)
edit_2b_line_71_normal_step_6.rationale: Step 6 wrote analysis records (large JSONs) to monitor/analysis/. Re-target to a canonical-namespace subdirectory monitor/analyst/analysis-records/ that workspace-sync's universal-pusher will pick up. Migration plan: any existing files at monitor/analysis/*.json should be moved to monitor/analyst/analysis-records/*.json as a one-time bash mv (see migration_plan section below).

edit_2c_line_153_normal_step_7.old_verbatim:
## Step 7: Write Summary

Write to `monitor/analysis/latest-analysis-summary.txt`. Update `status.json`.
edit_2c_line_153_normal_step_7.new_verbatim:
## Step 7: (Removed — superseded by analyst.md postlude)

Normal mode used to write its own summary here. As of PROP-036, the analyst.md postlude writes the summary unconditionally for every mode (including Normal). Normal-specific verbosity (e.g., 'CONSUMED: chg-XXX', 'KEY FINDING #N') belongs in the Step 6 analysis record JSON, not in the per-run summary text file.
edit_2c_line_153_normal_step_7.rationale: Removes the Normal-only summary write that was both wrong-pathed AND duplicative with the postlude. Mode-specific content moves to the analysis-record JSON where it belongs.

edit_3_decider_intake_md_path_fix.new_verbatim: - `monitor/analyst/latest-analysis-summary.txt` — latest analyst findings (sentinel; substantive analyst output is in monitor/analyst/expansions/ and monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/)
edit_3_decider_intake_md_path_fix.rationale: Decider was reading the typo path — has been seeing 19-day-stale content. Re-target to canonical; clarify that this is a human-readable sentinel, not the substantive output.

edit_4a_line_77.rationale: The Mode 2 error-pattern grep was scanning a FUSE-orphan directory — zero matches every run. Re-target to the canonical analysis-records location (see edit_2b).

edit_4b_line_86.rationale: Same fix as edit_4a, on the fallback grep block.

edit_5_tinker_pipeline_health_md_cross_c.operation: ADD new section after the existing PROP-034 Phase 1 baby pipeline health check (after line 12)
edit_5_tinker_pipeline_health_md_cross_c.new_block_verbatim:
**PROP-036 — Analyst sentinel + substantive-output cross-check (added 2026-05-15):** The analyst sentinel `monitor/analyst/latest-analysis-summary.txt` is now postlude-written unconditionally every run (PROP-036 edit_1). Probe its mtime against analyst's BAU cadence: if older than 16h (2× current BAU 8h interval) AND substantive-output mtimes (newest of monitor/analyst/expansions/*.json, monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/proposal-*.json) are ALSO older than 16h, flag as fully stalled. If sentinel is stale BUT substantive output is fresh, flag as `sentinel-broken-agent-healthy` (the failure mode PROP-036 was built to detect — broken postlude). If sentinel is fresh BUT substantive output is older than 2× cadence, flag as `sentinel-only-fresh` (the symmetric failure mode — postlude works but real work pipeline is broken). Apply the same cross-check to baby: sentinel is `monitor/analyst-baby/latest-baby-summary.txt` (PROP-034 Phase 1), substantive outputs are monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-*-baby-batch-*.json + issue-proposals authored_by='analyst-baby'.

Command template:
```bash
NOW_TS=$(date -u +%s)
# Analyst sentinel
A_SENT=$(stat -c %Y monitor/analyst/latest-analysis-summary.txt 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
A_SENT_AGE_H=$(( (NOW_TS - A_SENT) / 3600 ))
# Analyst substantive newest
A_SUB=$(ls -t monitor/analyst/expansions/*.json monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/proposal-*.json 2>/dev/null | head -1 | xargs -I{} stat -c %Y {} 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
A_SUB_AGE_H=$(( (NOW_TS - A_SUB) / 3600 ))
if [ $A_SENT_AGE_H -ge 16 ] && [ $A_SUB_AGE_H -ge 16 ]; then echo "ANALYST FULLY STALLED ($A_SENT_AGE_H h sentinel, $A_SUB_AGE_H h substantive)"; fi
if [ $A_SENT_AGE_H -ge 16 ] && [ $A_SUB_AGE_H -lt 16 ]; then echo "ANALYST sentinel-broken-agent-healthy ($A_SENT_AGE_H h sentinel, $A_SUB_AGE_H h substantive) — PROP-036 postlude write may be broken"; fi
if [ $A_SENT_AGE_H -lt 16 ] && [ $A_SUB_AGE_H -ge 16 ]; then echo "ANALYST sentinel-only-fresh ($A_SENT_AGE_H h sentinel, $A_SUB_AGE_H h substantive) — work pipeline may be broken behind a still-firing postlude"; fi
```

Also add `monitor/analyst/latest-analysis-summary.txt` to the Mode 1 staleness probe list in `monitor/prompts/tinker.md` line 121-125:
```bash
for f in monitor/changes/latest-poll-summary.txt monitor/curmudgeon/tracker.json monitor/decisions/daily-report-*.json monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json monitor/analyst/latest-analysis-summary.txt; do
  if [ -f "$f" ]; then echo "$(stat -c %Y "$f" 2>/dev/null || echo 0) $f"; fi
done | sort -n | head -5
```
edit_5_tinker_pipeline_health_md_cross_c.rationale: Closes the 19-day blind-spot that motivated this PROP. Defense in depth: probe both sentinel mtime AND substantive output mtime; flag asymmetries either direction so the next broken-sentinel bug surfaces in one tinker cycle, and the symmetric broken-work-pipeline-behind-fresh-sentinel bug ALSO surfaces in one cycle. The 16h threshold (2× current 8h BAU) is conservative — analyst runs more often than 8h under churn-and-burn (30m), so 16h is a comfortable 'definitely stalled' threshold without churn-and-burn false positives.

migration_plan_one_time_bash.purpose: Once the prompt edits land, move the 26 existing files under monitor/analysis/ to monitor/analyst/analysis-records/ so the historical analysis records remain accessible. The typo dir can then be deleted from FUSE.
migration_plan_one_time_bash.operator_executes_via_workspace_path_in_:
```bash
WORKSPACE=$(find /sessions/*/mnt/dome-model-review -maxdepth 0 2>/dev/null | head -1)
mkdir -p "${WORKSPACE}/monitor/analyst/analysis-records"
mv "${WORKSPACE}/monitor/analysis/"*.json "${WORKSPACE}/monitor/analyst/analysis-records/" 2>/dev/null
mv "${WORKSPACE}/monitor/analysis/"*.txt "${WORKSPACE}/monitor/analyst/analysis-records/" 2>/dev/null
# FUSE cannot unlink files — leave the empty typo dir; workspace-sync will not push monitor/analysis/ anyway. The empty dir is harmless.
ls "${WORKSPACE}/monitor/analyst/analysis-records/" | wc -l  # confirm 26 files moved
```
migration_plan_one_time_bash.rationale: Preserves the historical analysis records (some are referenced in CLAUDE.md timestamp lore and may have audit value). FUSE-cannot-unlink means the empty typo dir lingers; that's fine because (a) no prompt references it after the edits land, (b) workspace-sync doesn't push it, (c) it has no content to be confused by.

verification_pattern.purpose: After the prompt edits land and the next 2 analyst runs complete, tinker (or operator) verifies the fix held.

checks.name: sentinel_freshness
checks.pass_condition: mtime is within the last 16h (2× BAU cadence). After 2 analyst runs post-edit, mtime should be within the last 8h.
checks.fail_action: Postlude failed to write — inspect analyst's last-run output for postlude-step errors.

checks.name: sentinel_path_no_typo_reference
checks.pass_condition: Zero output — no remaining references to the typo path in any prompt file.
checks.fail_action: Path-typo edit didn't take in one of the three files; re-apply.

checks.name: sentinel_content_schema
checks.pass_condition: Content includes '=== Analyst run <ISO-TS> ===' header and 'Mode(s) fired:' line per Step P1 schema.
checks.fail_action: Postlude wrote a sentinel but content doesn't match the schema — analyst LLM may have improvised. Re-emphasize schema in edit_1.

checks.name: tinker_cross_check_lights_up_if_postlude_breaks
checks.command: Inject a deliberate one-cycle break: rename monitor/analyst/latest-analysis-summary.txt to .broken, run one tinker cycle, expect 'ANALYST sentinel-broken-agent-healthy' in the report's findings.
checks.pass_condition: Tinker run report's findings[] contains the sentinel-broken-agent-healthy finding within one tinker cycle.
checks.fail_action: Cross-check didn't fire — inspect tinker-pipeline-health.md for the PROP-036 block; verify thresholds.
checks.note: Optional smoke test, recommended within first week of fix landing.

checks.name: migration_completed
checks.fail_action: Migration bash didn't run; re-execute migration_plan_one_time_bash.

rollback_plan.purpose: If the postlude has unintended consequences (e.g., overwrites a sentinel that another agent depended on in an undocumented way), tinker or operator can revert.
steps: Revert the analyst.md postlude edit by replacing the new block with the original two-line 'After mode work completes' text.
Revert the analyst-normal-analysis.md path edits by restoring 'monitor/analysis/' in lines 7, 71, 153.
Leave the migrated files in monitor/analyst/analysis-records/ — no need to move them back; they're harmless in their new location.
rollback_plan.rollback_cost: Three commits or one commit reverting the bundle. ~5 minutes of operator time. The migrated analysis records stay put either way.

phased_implementation_plan.phase_1: Operator reviews this PROP. Approve / amend / reject. If approved, choose: (a) tinker self-applies under PROP-014-amendment-001 Q6 lifecycle, or (b) operator routes via a new directive that tinker dispatches against.
phased_implementation_plan.phase_2: Tinker (or operator) applies the 5 prompt edits in one commit and runs the migration bash. Decider will pick up the new path on its next run (no decider-side change required beyond the decider-intake.md edit).
phased_implementation_plan.phase_3: Operator monitors for 48h: tinker's next 2 runs should see a fresh sentinel and the substantive cross-check should not fire. Analyst's next 5-10 runs should each leave a fresh sentinel covering whatever mode(s) fired.
phased_implementation_plan.phase_4: If phase 3 is clean, PROP-036 is closed. If not, rollback per rollback_plan and re-investigate.

out_of_scope_for_this_prop: Cadence changes for the analyst or baby — both schedules are unchanged.
Analyst Mode N additions or removals — purely a write-discipline fix.
Decider-side changes other than the one-line decider-intake.md path correction.
Removing the empty monitor/analysis/ FUSE-orphan directory — FUSE doesn't support unlink and the empty dir is harmless after the prompt edits land.
Any change to PROP-034 Phase 1 baby cadence or model — baby's summary contract is already correct.
expected_artifacts: Tinker report file: monitor/tinker/report-<ts>.json with findings[] entry referencing this PROP
Tinker summary: monitor/tinker/latest-tinker-summary.txt with one-line note referencing this PROP

Directive completion fields appended to monitor/tinker/operator-directives/DIRECTIVE-20260514-001-analyst-summary-write-gap.json (status=completed, completed_at, completed_by_run, prop_id_authored=PROP-036)
